themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are nearly unanimous on the product, the Liège waffle and house ice cream combination draws direct comparisons to Belgium, but a recurring complaint about aggressive sample-limiting by staff has surfaced in multiple reviews and rattled an otherwise pristine record.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-two Pittsburgh creators have filmed at Treat this quarter, generating over 1.1 million TikTok plays, including a single video from @dipitcafe that cleared 296,000 views on a caption that was essentially just 'waffle with ice cream.' The business has not posted to its own Instagram in roughly 200 days. That gap is the editorial story of this issue for Treat: a Shadyside shop with a visually specific product, liege waffles stacked with handmade ice cream, is one of the most-filmed dessert stops in the city right now, and it has no mechanism to retain that attention. This quarter's Sweets family leader, Page's, owns its position on social momentum it controls. Treat's path from Ones to Watch toward that tier runs directly through reclaiming a feed that the city is already filling in for it.
tldr_read: Over a million TikTok plays from creators who aren't the business, a dormant Instagram, and a near-perfect review record. Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The product signal and creator coverage are already at the level of a higher tier; the rank ceiling is the Instagram silence, which is the one axis where Treat trails the family median by the widest margin.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Third In Ice Cream, Carried By 32 Creators. Its Own Feed Has Been Quiet For 200 Days.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram feed, now
playbook.action: The account has not posted in 200 days despite a 7.65% engagement rate; one post per week would re-anchor an audience already proven to care.

playbook.headline: Publish a clear sample policy in-store
playbook.action: Two reviews this quarter cite the same staff friction around samples; a posted limit prevents the exchange before it starts and protects a 98% five-star record.

playbook.headline: Tag the creators already filming here
playbook.action: Thirty-two creators produced 1.1 million plays this quarter; resharing even a fraction of that on the Instagram account converts passive coverage into a recoverable visual catalog.
